title: Fit a Hierarchical Bayes mixture model via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC
summary: Estimate a Hierarchical Bayes model via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC from coded-long-format choice data. Produces individual-level (respondent) preference estimates, segment-level summaries, and fit statistics. Supports multi-segment mixture models, holdout evaluation, and respondent-level covariates.
description: Reads a coded long-format choice CSV (the output of `choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long`) and fits a Hierarchical Bayes (HB) mixture model by Metropolis-Hastings MCMC. The model treats each respondent as having their own utility vector, drawn from a population-level normal mixture with `segments` components. The result is a per-respondent posterior over partworths plus segment-level summaries — the standard HB output for choice modelling. Optional features include an out-of-sample holdout (`test_input`), respondent-level covariates (`covariate_input`), sign constraints, and chain checkpointing.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::fit"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
# optional:
test_input = "<holdout.csv>"
covariate_input = "<covariates.csv>"
segments = 2
iterations = 10000
save_every = 10
threads = 4
random_seed = 0
burnin = 0.2
attribute_signs = [{ name = "price", rule = "non_pos" }]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV file. Expected layout: one row per (respondent, task, alternative), with a `resp_id` column, a `task` column, a response column, and one numeric column per coded covariate. This is exactly what `choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long` produces.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives the persisted chain (chunks of saved draws), `spec.json` recording the run configuration, `manifest.postcard` and `status.postcard` for chain bookkeeping, and the `exports/` subdirectory with user-facing CSVs after `finalize` is run (or automatically at end of fit).

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: test_input
arguments.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation. Must have the same column layout as `input`. When supplied, the run records the holdout log-likelihood under the post-burnin draws as part of the fit summary. Use this when you want a single train / test split; for K-fold cross-validation, use `cv-fit` instead.

arguments.name: covariate_input
arguments.description: Optional respondent-level covariate CSV: a `resp_id` column plus one numeric column per covariate. When supplied, the population-level mean is regressed on these covariates (one regression coefficient per covariate per partworth). Use this to capture demographic effects on preferences when you have respondent metadata.

arguments.name: segments
arguments.description: Number of mixture components in the population-level normal mixture. Default `2`. With `segments = 1` the model is a standard single-population HB; with `segments > 1` it is a normal-mixture HB, useful when the population is meaningfully multi-modal. Increasing `segments` adds parameters and slows convergence, so start small.

arguments.name: iterations
arguments.description: Total MCMC iterations to run. Default `10000`. The chain after burnin is what `finalize` summarises; run more iterations if the trace plots or fit statistics suggest the chain hasn't mixed. If a run undershoots, use `fit-extend` to add more iterations on top of the existing chain rather than starting over.

arguments.name: save_every
arguments.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default `10` — i.e., save 1 in 10 iterations. Larger values save fewer draws but produce a thinner chain in the persisted output, which keeps disk usage manageable on long runs. Holdout evaluation and final summaries operate on saved draws only.

arguments.name: show_every
arguments.description: Print progress every N iterations. Default `50`. Has no effect on results — purely a console-output knob.

arguments.name: checkpoint_every
arguments.description: Write a chain checkpoint every N saved draws. Default `100`. Checkpoints make `fit-extend` resumption robust to mid-run interruptions.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of threads used for the per-respondent likelihood evaluations inside each MCMC step. Default `4`. Set to roughly the number of cores you can spare. Speedup is sub-linear past about 8 threads on most respondent counts.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed. `0` (default) means non-deterministic — a fresh seed is drawn each call. Provide a positive integer for reproducibility; the same seed and the same input give bit-identical chains.

arguments.name: chains
arguments.description: Number of independent MCMC chains to run in parallel. Default `1`. Each chain uses a seed derived from `random_seed` and its chain index. Chains run concurrently; their draws are pooled at finalize time to form the final point estimates and summaries. Use `chains > 1` to assess convergence across independent starting points or to increase effective posterior sample size.

arguments.name: report_thin
arguments.description: Convergence-report snapshot interval in saved draws. Default `0` (same as `checkpoint_every`, giving one snapshot per chunk). A snapshot of the running cumulative mean is written to `process_data.json` every N saved draws, producing one data point on the convergence chart. Lower values (e.g. `10`) give a finer trace at the cost of more frequent file writes; higher values give a coarser trace with fewer data points. Has no effect on the MCMC chain itself.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction discarded from the saved draws when computing the holdout log-likelihood and the final summary. Default `0.2` (discard the first 20% of saved draws). The burnin can be changed retroactively by re-running `finalize` with a different value — the persisted chain itself is unaffected.

arguments.name: amu
arguments.description: Precision of the normal prior on each segment's mean partworth vector. Default `0.01` is very weak (sd ≈ 10); use a tighter value if your data is sparse and the chain wanders.

arguments.name: nu_inc
arguments.description: Degrees-of-freedom increment for the inverse-Wishart prior on each segment's covariance matrix. The actual prior degrees of freedom are `n_attributes + nu_inc`. Default `3` is the conventional weak choice.

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: Dirichlet concentration parameters for the segment mixture weights. Length must equal `segments`. When omitted, defaults to a symmetric Dirichlet (all entries equal) consistent with a weak prior.

arguments.name: covariate_prior_scale
arguments.description: Prior scale for the regression coefficients on `covariate_input`. Default `0.01` is weak; tighten if you have many covariates and few respondents.

arguments.name: step_size
arguments.description: Metropolis-Hastings proposal step size for the per-respondent partworth updates. Omit (the default) for automatic tuning during burnin — the kernel adapts the step size to target a reasonable acceptance rate. Provide an explicit value only if automatic tuning misbehaves.

arguments.name: init_ridge
arguments.description: Numerical ridge added to the Hessian diagonal during the MAP initialisation step. Default `1e-8`. Raise if MAP init fails on a near-singular Hessian (e.g., a near-collinear coding scheme).

arguments.name: init_iterations
arguments.description: Maximum iterations for the MAP initialisation routine that seeds the chain. Default `250`. The chain converges much faster from a good MAP start than from a random one, so this is worth doing even on long runs.

arguments.name: attribute_signs
arguments.description: Sign constraints on individual attributes, formatted as a list of `{ name, rule }` objects where `rule` is `"non_neg"` (the partworth must be non-negative) or `"non_pos"` (must be non-positive). Use `non_pos` for price-like attributes you want to force downward-sloping. Sign constraints are enforced via rejection at the proposal stage.

details:
`fit` is the primary HB estimation entry point. It runs Metropolis-Hastings MCMC over a hierarchical normal-mixture model: each respondent has their own partworth vector drawn from a population-level normal mixture with `segments` components, and each task is a multinomial choice over alternatives whose linear utilities use that respondent's partworths.

The sampler is multi-threaded across respondents inside each MCMC step. Step size is adaptively tuned during burnin unless `step_size` is supplied explicitly. A MAP initialisation routine seeds the chain from a good starting point so the warm-up phase doesn't waste iterations wandering.

The persisted output is split into two layers. The *raw chain* (chunks of saved draws plus `spec.json`, `manifest.postcard`, `status.postcard`) lives in `run_dir` and is the durable record of the run. The *user-facing summary* (segment means, mixture probabilities, per-respondent point estimates) is produced by `finalize`, which can be re-run with different burnin values without re-running the chain. `fit` calls `finalize` automatically at the end of a successful run, so you get both layers in one call.

If the run undershoots (trace plots haven't settled, holdout LL is still climbing), use `fit-extend` to add more iterations on top of the existing chain rather than starting over.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:

- The persisted chain (`chunks/`, `spec.json`, `manifest.postcard`, `status.postcard`).
- An `exports/` subdirectory with `summary.json` (segment means, mixture probabilities, log-likelihood) and the per-respondent point-estimates CSV — these are produced by the auto-`finalize` step at the end of the run.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, periodic `event.run.progress` events with iteration counts, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the final fit summary.
notes: Use `cv-fit` for K-fold cross-validation rather than calling `fit` once per fold — it walks a `choice-cv split` directory and writes a single aggregate summary.
If a run hasn't converged, prefer `fit-extend` over re-running. Extending preserves the warm-up work and is much cheaper.
The holdout evaluation is computed against the post-burnin saved draws. To re-evaluate with a different burnin without re-running the chain, re-run `finalize`.
Sign constraints (`attribute_signs`) are enforced at the proposal stage; expect a slower acceptance rate when many constraints are active.

see_also.description: Add more iterations on top of an existing chain (use this when a fit hasn't converged).
K-fold cross-validation entry point — runs fit per fold against a `choice-cv split` directory.
Re-summarise a completed run with a different burnin value.
Produces the coded long-format CSV that this command consumes.
Aggregate MNL fit — much faster but ignores respondent-level heterogeneity.
Latent class fit — segments respondents but assigns each to a single class rather than a continuous individual posterior.

examples.title: 1-segment HB with sign constraint on price
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::fit"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-1seg"
run_name = "hbmh_run2"
segments = 1
iterations = 20000
attribute_signs = [{ name = "price", rule = "non_pos" }]
random_seed = 42

examples.title: HB with respondent covariates and a holdout
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::fit"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long_train.csv"
test_input = "./estimation/coded_long_holdout.csv"
covariate_input = "./estimation/respondent_covariates.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-cov"
run_name = "hbmh_run3"
segments = 2
iterations = 15000

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long-format CSV file.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: test_input
fields.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

fields.name: covariate_input
fields.description: Optional covariate CSV (resp_id + numeric columns).

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Number of mixture segments. Default: 2.

fields.name: iterations
fields.description: Total MCMC iterations. Default: 10000.

fields.name: save_every
fields.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default: 10.

fields.name: show_every
fields.description: Print progress every N iterations. Default: 50.

fields.name: checkpoint_every
fields.description: Write checkpoint every N saved draws. Default: 100.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Number of threads. Default: 4.

fields.name: random_seed
fields.description: Random seed (0 = non-deterministic). Default: 0.

fields.name: chains
fields.description: Number of independent MCMC chains. Default: 1.

fields.name: report_thin
fields.description: Snapshot every N saved draws (0 = checkpoint_every). Default: 0.

fields.name: burnin
fields.description: Burnin fraction for holdout evaluation. Default: 0.2.

fields.name: amu
fields.description: Precision of normal prior on segment means. Default: 0.01.

fields.name: nu_inc
fields.description: Degrees-of-freedom increment for covariance prior. Default: 3.

fields.name: a
fields.description: Dirichlet concentration per segment (length = segments).

fields.name: covariate_prior_scale
fields.description: Prior scale for covariate regression coefficients. Default: 0.01.

fields.name: step_size
fields.description: MH proposal step size. Omit for automatic tuning.

fields.name: init_ridge
fields.description: Numerical ridge for Hessian during MAP init. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: init_iterations
fields.description: Max iterations for MAP initialisation. Default: 250.

fields.name: attribute_signs
fields.description: Sign constraints: [{name, rule}] where rule is non_neg or non_pos.

artifacts.title: Model fit summary
artifacts.description: Segment-level means, mixture probabilities, and log-likelihood statistics for the fitted model. Includes training log-likelihood and — if a holdout file was supplied — the out-of-sample holdout log-likelihood for model comparison.

artifacts.title: Individual part-worth utilities
artifacts.description: The key output file. One row per respondent with their posterior-mean preference estimates for each attribute. Feed this to sim_db_build (model_type=hb) to build a market simulator for competitive-share or take-rate projections.

fields.name: resp_id
fields.description: Respondent identifier.

fields.name: (attribute columns)
fields.description: Posterior-mean part-worth utility for each coded attribute. One column per attribute in the coded-long input.
